concepts.name: Matrix Multiplication Workloads
concepts.description: The core linear algebra operations that dominate computational time and determine if a neural network layer is limited by compute capabilities or memory bandwidth.

concepts.name: The Silicon Contract
concepts.description: The computational bargain an architecture makes with hardware, dictating exact memory consumption, execution time, and energy expenditure.

concepts.name: The Memory Wall
concepts.description: The fundamental hardware bottleneck where memory bandwidth scaling lags behind processing capacity, causing data movement to throttle overall computational throughput.

concepts.name: Data Movement Energy Cost
concepts.description: The physical constraint where transferring model parameters and activations between memory and processing units consumes orders of magnitude more power than the actual mathematical operations.

concepts.name: Multiply-Accumulate Operations
concepts.description: The foundational hardware-level arithmetic instruction that computes the weighted sums of neural inputs and defines the theoretical maximum throughput of an AI accelerator.

concepts.name: Activation Logic Unit Cost
concepts.description: The physical silicon area and transistor count required to implement a nonlinear activation function in hardware, favoring simple operators like ReLU over complex exponentials.

concepts.name: Activation Memory Footprint
concepts.description: The massive memory capacity required during training to store all intermediate layer outputs from the forward pass for reuse in gradient computation during backpropagation.

concepts.name: Batch Processing Trade-offs
concepts.description: The system engineering balance where increasing the number of concurrent inputs improves parallel hardware utilization but scales the activation memory footprint linearly.

concepts.name: Dense Connectivity Scaling
concepts.description: An architectural pattern where full connections between layers cause the parameter count and resulting memory storage requirements to scale quadratically with layer width.

concepts.name: Activation Sparsity
concepts.description: The property introduced by specific non-linearities that zero out negative inputs, reducing the computational memory footprint and energy usage by deactivating parts of the network dynamically.

concepts.name: Dying ReLU Problem
concepts.description: A state where a significant percentage of a network's neurons permanently output zero and cease learning, resulting in wasted computational capacity and inefficient memory usage.

concepts.name: Vanishing Gradient Problem
concepts.description: A mathematical failure mode where error signals shrink exponentially through deep layers due to saturated activation functions, silently preventing early layers from updating.

concepts.name: Credit Assignment Problem
concepts.description: The fundamental algorithmic challenge of calculating exactly how much each of the millions of individual weight parameters contributed to the final network prediction error.

concepts.name: Double Descent Phenomenon
concepts.description: The counterintuitive scaling behavior where massive model overparameterization paradoxically improves generalization and reduces test error beyond traditional statistical limits.
